questions.text: What was the term used for big landlords or warrior chiefs by existing kings in the 7th century?
questions.explanation: Existing kings acknowledged warrior chiefs as samantas, expecting them to bring gifts and provide military support.

questions.text: The Rashtrakutas were initially subordinate to the:
questions.explanation: In the mid-8th century, Dantidurga, a Rashtrakuta chief, overthrew his Chalukya overlord.

questions.text: The 'Hiranya-garbha' ritual was performed to:
options: Become a 'Kshatriya' even if not one by birth
questions.explanation: Literally meaning the 'golden womb', this ritual symbolized the rebirth of the sacrificer as a Kshatriya.

questions.text: Which of these Brahmanas gave up their traditional professions to establish kingdoms?
questions.explanation: Kadamba Mayurasharman (Karnataka) and Gurjara-Pratihara Harichandra (Rajasthan) successfully established kingdoms.

questions.text: What was the 'vetti' tax under the Cholas?
questions.explanation: Vetti was a tax taken not in cash but in the form of forced labour.

questions.text: The inscriptions of the Cholas refer to more than ________ terms for different kinds of taxes.
questions.explanation: The Chola empire had a highly organized and detailed taxation system.

questions.text: Prashastis were composed by:
questions.explanation: Prashastis contained details that might not be literally true but showed how rulers wanted to depict themselves.

questions.text: Who was the author of the long Sanskrit poem containing the history of kings of Kashmir?
questions.explanation: Kalhana's 'Rajatarangini' was unusual because it was critical about rulers and their policies.

questions.text: The 'tripartite struggle' was fought over the city of:
questions.explanation: The Gurjara-Pratihara, Rashtrakuta, and Pala dynasties fought for centuries for control over Kanauj.

questions.text: Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni was from:
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni raided the subcontinent 17 times to plunder wealth from wealthy temples.

questions.text: Which famous temple was raided by Mahmud of Ghazni?
questions.explanation: The Somnath temple in Gujarat was a major target of Mahmud's raids.

questions.text: Who wrote 'Kitab al-Hind'?
questions.explanation: Al-Biruni was commissioned by Sultan Mahmud to write an account of the subcontinent.

questions.text: The Chahamanas, later known as Chauhans, ruled over the region around:
questions.explanation: The Chauhans attempted to expand their control and clashed with the Chalukyas and Gahadavalas.

questions.text: Prithviraja III defeated the Afghan ruler Sultan Muhammad Ghori in:
questions.explanation: He defeated Ghori in the first battle of Tarain in 1191 but lost to him the very next year.

questions.text: The Chola dynasty was founded by ________ who captured Tanjavur.
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya, from the ancient family of the Cholas from Uraiyur, captured the Kaveri delta.

questions.text: Which Chola ruler built the temple of Brihadishvara at Tanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I was the most powerful Chola ruler and a great builder of temples.

questions.text: The Chola king who developed a powerful navy and raided Southeast Asia was:
questions.explanation: Rajendra I expanded the empire and launched naval expeditions across the Bay of Bengal.

questions.text: Chola temples were not only places of worship but also hubs of:
questions.explanation: Temples were nuclei of settlements and centers of craft production, including bronze images.

questions.text: The most distinctive craft associated with Chola temples was the making of:
questions.explanation: Chola bronze images are considered amongst the finest in the world.

questions.text: The settlements of peasants in the Chola empire were known as:
questions.explanation: Ur were settlements of peasants that became prosperous with irrigation agriculture.

questions.text: Groups of villages in the Chola empire formed larger units called:
questions.explanation: The Nadu performed administrative functions including justice and tax collection.

questions.text: The land given to Brahmanas was known as:
questions.explanation: As a result of brahmadeya, large numbers of Brahmana settlements emerged in the Kaveri valley.

questions.text: The assembly of prominent Brahmana landholders was called the:
questions.explanation: The Sabha looked after the affairs of the brahmadeya and recorded its decisions in inscriptions.

questions.text: Associations of traders were known as:
questions.explanation: Nagarams occasionally performed administrative functions in towns.

questions.text: Detailed inscriptions from ________ provide information on the organization of the Sabha.
questions.explanation: Inscriptions from Uttiramerur in Tamil Nadu describe how the Sabha was organized and how members were chosen.

questions.text: How were members of the Sabha committees chosen?
questions.explanation: Names of eligible persons were written on palm leaf tickets and put into a pot; a young boy then picked them.

questions.text: What was 'Shalabhoga'?
options: Land for the maintenance of a school
questions.explanation: Shalabhoga was one of the five categories of land mentioned in Chola inscriptions.

questions.text: Rich peasants of the ________ caste exercised considerable control over the Nadu.
questions.explanation: The Vellala peasants were supervised by the central Chola government.

questions.text: Titles like 'Muvendavelan' were given to:
options: Rich landowners who served three kings
questions.explanation: These titles were marks of respect given by Chola kings to rich landowners.

questions.text: The Rashtrakutas rose to power in which region?
questions.explanation: They became the primary power in the Deccan after overthrowing the Chalukyas.

questions.text: Which clan did Nagabhata belong to?
questions.explanation: Nagabhata was a famous king whose achievements were recorded in a Gwalior prashasti.

questions.text: The Tang dynasty ruled in:
questions.explanation: The Tang dynasty in China remained in power for about 300 years.

questions.text: Maharaja-adhiraja means:
questions.explanation: New kings often took high-sounding titles to assert their authority.

questions.text: Resources for the state were collected from:
questions.explanation: Producers were often persuaded or compelled to surrender part of what they produced as 'rent'.

questions.text: Who was the founder of the Pala dynasty?
questions.explanation: Gopala founded the Pala dynasty, which ruled mainly over Bengal.

questions.text: In the Chola administration, the royal orders were recorded by the:
questions.explanation: Olai was the designation of the officer who recorded royal oral orders.

questions.text: The land for Jain institutions was called:
questions.explanation: Pallichchhandam is a term found in Chola inscriptions for Jain land grants.

questions.text: Which dynasty was involved in the tripartite struggle from the East?
questions.explanation: The Palas ruled from Bengal in the East.

questions.text: Temple of Gangaikondacholapuram was built by:
questions.explanation: Rajendra I built this temple to celebrate his victory in the North (Ganges expedition).

questions.text: The main purpose of the military raids of rulers during this period was to:
options: Acquire wealth, especially in the form of temple treasures
questions.explanation: Rulers targeted temples because they were centers of immense wealth.
